tldr: The tape is bifurcated: software (NOW, WDAY, DDOG, VEEV) is ripping on AI-agent product cycles and earnings beats, while semis/storage (ON, WDC, STX, SNDK, KEYS, MPWR) are getting hit hard on deal dilution fears and post-earnings fatigue. The single most important thing is ON Semiconductor's -23.7% plunge on the Synaptics acquisition announcement — a deal that is re-rating the acquirer and dragging the entire semi complex lower.

sections.heading: Software AI-Agent Rally: Real Earnings, Real Product Cycles
sections.synthesis: The software complex is rallying on actual reported results and concrete AI-agent product launches — this looks like genuine earnings-driven re-rating, not just sentiment.

bullets.fact: NOW +9.9% on Q1 2026 earnings report; WDAY +9.2% on FQ1 2027 results; DDOG +8.5% on Q1 2026 results with Truist upgrade to Buy (PT 300, +25% upside); VEEV +8.4% on Q4/FY2026 results and Falcon agentic platform launch.
bullets.read: All four movers have confirmed earnings catalysts plus AI-agent product narratives (NOW's Knowledge 2026 partner ecosystem, WDAY's developer tools for AI agents, DDOG's Bits AI security analyst, VEEV's Falcon platform). The convergence of beats + product launches + analyst upgrades (DDOG) suggests the market is rewarding SaaS names that can point to agentic AI as a revenue vector. DDOG at fwdP/E 84 with +76% YTD and only -14% from highs has the most room to run if AI monetization accelerates; WDAY at fwdP/E 10 and -50% from highs may be the deepest value bounce. Watch whether NOW and WDAY can hold these levels — both are still down 35-50% YTD, so this could be dead-cat or inflection. Confirmation: next quarter's AI-related ARR disclosure.

bullets.fact: FDS +11.0% with no earnings catalyst; recent headlines reference Anthropic's AI agents for financial services tasks and FactSet outperforming/underperforming competitors on successive days.
bullets.read: The Anthropic AI-agent headline is the most plausible driver — FactSet is a financial-data platform that could be either disrupted or enhanced by AI agents fielding financial-services tasks. An 11% move on that headline alone is aggressive for a mid-cap with -20% YTD and -49% from highs, suggesting either short covering or a speculative AI-premium re-rating. The competing headlines (outperforming one day, underperforming the next) signal no clear fundamental shift. This has lower signal quality than the NOW/WDAY/DDOG/VEEV group. Reports July 1 — the earnings print will confirm or refute the AI narrative. Treat as positioning ahead of catalyst.

sections.heading: Semis/Storage Selloff: Deal Dilution + Post-Earnings Fatigue
sections.synthesis: The entire semi/storage complex is rolling over simultaneously — ON's deal announcement is the anchor, but the breadth of weakness (WDC, STX, SNDK, KEYS, MPWR) suggests something broader than a single deal.

bullets.fact: ON -23.7% on announced acquisition of Synaptics; 425 filings from both ON and SYNA confirm proposed business combination; no deal terms disclosed yet.
bullets.read: A -23.7% one-day move on an acquisition announcement is a brutal acquirer penalty — the market is pricing in either a large dilutive equity component, strategic skepticism about ON-SYNA synergies, or both. The 425 filings explicitly state terms will come in the forthcoming S-4, so the uncertainty is itself the driver. ON was +67% YTD coming in, so this is a sharp momentum reversal at scale. The read-through: if ON is using stock, it signals management thinks the stock is rich; if cash, balance sheet stress. Watch the S-4 for structure. SYNA holders face the inverse — a takeout premium with closing risk. The -25.5% one-week move suggests the selling is not done.

bullets.fact: WDC -13.2%, STX -12.2%, SNDK -10.5% — all storage names down double digits despite Morgan Stanley naming WDC a top pick; STX and SNDK both reported FQ3 2026 results recently; SNDK also announced exchangeable note exchanges.
bullets.read: Three storage names falling 10-13% simultaneously, with SNDK at +781% YTD and WDC/STX at +227-240% YTD, has the hallmarks of a crowded-trade unwind after a massive run. STX and SNDK both have recent earnings (FQ3 2026) — the note exchanges at STX/SNDK are balance-sheet management, not a bearish signal per se, but they add to the noise. The Morgan Stanley top-pick headline on WDC couldn't stop the slide, meaning the sell-side is now lagging the tape. The key question: is this flash-crash profit-taking in a over-extended group, or the start of a fundamental top in the storage cycle? SNDK at fwdP/E 11 is the cheapest and closest to highs (-11%), suggesting the market still believes in earnings power. Watch whether the group stabilizes or if the selling bleeds into tomorrow.

bullets.fact: KEYS -8.7% on Q2 2026 results; MPWR -8.7% with shareholder investigation announcements (Schall Law Firm, Rosen Law Firm) and a patent infringement win.
bullets.read: KEYS weakness is straightforward post-earnings reaction — the Liquid Instruments Series C co-investment is a strategic positive but not enough to offset results. MPWR is more complex: securities litigation firms announcing investigations is typically ambulance-chasing after a price drop, not a cause. The patent infringement *win* is actually positive. The real driver is likely the ON-Synaptics deal read-through — MPWR is a power management analog/semi name, and the ON deal signals potential M&A disruption in the analog/power space. MPWR at fwdP/E 43 and -23% from highs was already de-rating before today. Watch for whether the investigation announcements trigger further selling or fade as noise.

sections.heading: MRNA: Flu Vaccine Data + Patent Settlement
sections.synthesis: Moderna's 12.6% surge is the clearest single-name catalyst-driven move of the session — NEJM publication of Phase 3 flu data plus a global patent litigation resolution de-risk two overhangs simultaneously.

bullets.fact: MRNA +12.6% on NEJM publication of pivotal Phase 3 data for mRNA-1010 seasonal flu vaccine and resolution of global patent litigation with Arbutus/Genevant; also announced data at 2026 ASCO.
bullets.read: This is a genuine dual-catalyst move: NEJM publication of Phase 3 flu data is a credibility milestone for mRNA-1010, and the Arbutus/Genevant patent settlement removes a legal overhang that has capped the stock. With +128% YTD and only -2.9% from the 52-week high, MRNA is at the upper end of its range — the market is pricing in a successful pivot from COVID to a broader infectious-disease platform. The ASCO data mention is a secondary read-through to the oncology pipeline. The risk: at fwdP/E of -16 (negative earnings), this is a momentum/story stock where sentiment can reverse fast if flu vaccine regulatory timelines slip. Watch for FDA interaction on mRNA-1010 and any commercialization timeline guidance.

sections.heading: Deal Flow: Take-Private Votes, M&A Approvals, and Capital Raising
sections.synthesis: The 8-K flood shows a deal-making wave: take-private votes clearing (SEM, AES), strategic acquisitions advancing (NFG/Vectren, UUUU/VAC, VTOL/Berry, FUL/AMS), and large debt issuance (IRM, HASI) — the market is open for risk and capital deployment.

bullets.fact: SEM stockholders approved take-private by Ortenzio/Jackson/WCAS consortium (82.5% voted, >79.9% of outstanding approved); AES stockholders approved $15.00/sh GIP-EQT buyout (~97.9% of votes in favor).
bullets.read: Both take-private votes cleared decisively — SEM at >79.9% of outstanding and AES at ~67% of outstanding. The principal deal-contingent risk (shareholder approval) is now removed for both, leaving only regulatory and customary closing conditions. SEM expects mid-2026 close; AES targets late 2026/early 2027. For deal-arb players, the spread should compress to a regulatory/time-value discount. The AES vote at $15.00 is notable because it's a mega-cap infrastructure take-private — a signal that large-cap PE infrastructure capital is deploying at scale.

bullets.fact: NFG's $2.62B Vectren Ohio acquisition approved by PUCO (Q4 2026 close expected); UUUU files definitive agreement to acquire Vacuumschmelze for ~$718M cash plus shares; VTOL to acquire Berry Aviation for $105M all-cash; FUL secures Goldman bridge facility for Advanced Medical Solutions acquisition.
bullets.read: Four acquisitions across utilities (NFG), rare earths/magnets (UUUU), aviation services (VTOL), and medical adhesives (FUL) — the breadth signals open M&A markets across sectors. NFG's PUCO approval is the most de-risked (regulatory clearance obtained, ~35% of market cap). UUUU's VAC acquisition is the most transformative — moving into permanent magnet manufacturing and vertically integrating the rare earths value chain, but requiring significant capital and regulatory approvals. VTOL's Berry deal is small ($105M) but strategically sharp: exiting Norway offshore, adding U.S. government/defense revenue (72% of Berry's $108M revenue). FUL's AMS acquisition adds medical adhesives but layers leverage on top of $1.96B existing net debt — watch for the permanent financing plan.

bullets.fact: IRM prices $1.5B 6.250% senior notes due 2035; HASI prices $1.0B 5.95% green senior notes due 2033; APLD enters new Goldman-led syndicated credit facility to refinance existing debt.
bullets.read: Three sizable debt issuances — IRM ($1.5B at 6.25%), HASI ($1B at 5.95%, ~20% of market cap), and APLD (Goldman-led syndicated refinance). The takeaway: credit markets are open and functioning for leveraged companies. IRM is extending maturities but increasing interest costs on an already leveraged balance sheet — the bull case is data-center demand driving storage revenue; the bear case is leverage compression. HASI's 5.95% on 7-year green notes funds its sustainable-infrastructure pipeline at a known cost. APLD's Goldman-led refinance replaces FNBO and Macquarie facilities — improved lender access is positive but pricing is redacted, so terms are unknown.

sections.heading: Options Flow: SNDK Two-Way, NVDA Put-Heavy, MSFT/AAPL Call-Heavy
sections.synthesis: Options flow is mixed but the standout patterns are: SNDK seeing massive two-way volume near highs, NVDA put-heavy on a down day, and MSFT/AAPL call-heavy on strength — positioning is split between semis caution and mega-cap tech optimism.

bullets.fact: SNDK: $151.5M total ($95.7M calls / $55.8M puts), biggest call $1500 3dte; stock -10.5%. MU: $285.8M total ($144.3M calls / $141.6M puts), biggest call $1200 3dte; stock -6.7%.
bullets.read: SNDK and MU are both seeing massive two-way flow with large near-dated call premiums (SNDK $1500 3dte, MU $1200 3dte) despite both stocks falling 6-10%. This is either: (a) bottom-fishing call buyers betting the selloff is overdone, or (b) call selling against long stock to finance downside protection. The near-dated nature (3dte) suggests event-driven positioning — SNDK just reported earnings, MU may have upcoming catalysts. The put volume is also substantial (SNDK $55.8M, MU $141.6M), indicating genuine two-way debate. With SNDK at +781% YTD and MU a semi name under pressure from the ON deal read-through, this is the key options signal to watch for direction in the storage/semi complex.

bullets.fact: NVDA: $27.8M total ($7.1M calls / $20.7M puts), biggest put $192.5 0dte; stock -1.6%. MSFT: $36.2M total ($32.1M calls / $4.1M puts), biggest call $370 0dte; stock +5.7%. AAPL: $33.1M total ($27.9M calls / $5.3M puts), biggest call $280 0dte; stock +3.1%.
bullets.read: NVDA's put-heavy flow on a modest -1.6% decline is notable — the $192.5 0dte put suggests near-term hedging or directional bearish bets, possibly tied to the broader semi weakness from ON/Synaptics. Meanwhile MSFT and AAPL are seeing call-heavy flow on strength (+5.7% and +3.1%), with 0dte calls suggesting momentum continuation bets. The divergence — NVDA puts vs. MSFT/AAPL calls — reflects a rotation narrative: capital moving from semis/AI hardware into software/AI applications. This aligns with the NOW/WDAY/DDOG/VEEV rally. AMD also call-heavy ($65.6M calls vs $19.7M puts) with a Citi upgrade to Buy (PT 575, +10%), bucking the semi weakness — the market is differentiating between AI-exposed semis (AMD bullish) and deal-disrupted semis (ON bearish).

sections.heading: Analyst Changes: Semi Upgrades vs. Software/Real Estate Downgrades
sections.synthesis: Analyst moves are bifurcated along the same lines as the tape: semis getting upgraded (QCOM, AMD, INTC) while software sees mixed action (DDOG upgraded, AAPL downgraded) and real estate gets cautious (PLD downgraded).

bullets.fact: Upgrades: QCOM UW→EW (MS, PT 231, +22%); AMD N→Buy (Citi, PT 575, +10%); INTC UW→Buy (BofA, PT 135, +5%); SNPS N→OW (Piper, PT 550, +21%); DDOG Hold→Buy (Truist, PT 300, +25%); IBM N→OW (JPM, PT 291, +7%); COP N→Buy (Roth, PT 130, +23%); XOM N→Buy (BofA, PT 154, +13%); PLTR UP→Peer Perform (Wolfe). Downgrades: HD OP→Peer Perform (Wolfe); AAPL OP→Hold (KGI, PT 315, +11%); PLD SO→SP (Scotia, PT 146, +4%).
bullets.read: The most notable pattern: three semi upgrades (QCOM, AMD, INTC) on the same day ON is cratering on the Synaptics deal — sell-side is calling the semi weakness a buying opportunity even as the market punishes the deal. INTC's upgrade from Underperform to Buy at BofA (PT 135, only +5% upside) is the most contrarian — a full reversal on a name that's been widely disliked. DDOG's Truist upgrade to Buy (PT 300, +25%) aligns with the earnings beat and AI-agent narrative. The PLD downgrade (Sector Outperform→Sector Perform, PT 146, only +4% upside) is a valuation call on industrial real estate after a big run. AAPL's KGI downgrade to Hold (PT 315, +11%) is a minor call but contrasts with the call-heavy options flow — the market is more bullish than the analyst. Watch whether the semi upgrades stick or if ON's deal overhang drags the complex further.

sections.heading: Single-Name Movers and Filings Watch
sections.synthesis: Smaller filings and single-name moves round out the tape — the standout is CAPR's FDA advisory committee scheduling and GT's abrupt CFO departure.

bullets.fact: CAPR 8-K: FDA Advisory Committee set for July 29 to review Deramiocel BLA for DMD; PDUFA date August 22, 2026.
bullets.read: The AdCom scheduling is a critical milestone — it keeps the BLA review on track and provides a concrete catalyst timeline. AdComs are binary events: a positive vote de-risks the PDUFA, a negative vote can sink the stock. The July 29 date gives investors a month to position. Watch for briefing documents (typically posted 1-2 days before AdCom) — those are the true catalyst.

bullets.fact: GT 8-K: CFO Zamarro departing June 30 with ~8 days notice; interim CFO Deakin appointed; departure stated as not related to financial results or disagreements.
bullets.read: An 8-day-notice CFO departure at a highly leveraged company is a yellow flag regardless of the 'no disagreement' language — that boilerplate is standard but the speed of the transition suggests urgency. Goodyear's balance sheet is leveraged, and a CFO transition creates uncertainty around capital allocation, debt management, and potential restructuring. The 'pursuing another opportunity' language is plausible but the timeline is tight. Watch for any Q2 earnings commentary or balance-sheet updates.

bullets.fact: PAHC names Dani Bendheim CEO effective July 1; 300K performance RSUs with vesting only above $70 stock price, 5-year cliff.
bullets.read: The RSU structure is the signal — vesting only above $70 with a 5-year cliff is an aggressive performance-linked grant that ties management compensation to substantial shareholder value creation. This is a governance positive if you believe in alignment, but it also signals the board sees $70 as a stretch target. Watch for Bendheim's strategic priorities at the next earnings call.

bullets.fact: ITGR 8-K: CRM & Neuromodulation President Stephens moves to interim special-projects role with March 2027 exit; ineligible for 2027 incentives.
bullets.read: A segment president being moved to a time-limited transitional role with no future incentives and a defined exit date is a quiet departure — the kind of organizational change that often precedes a segment sale or restructuring. Integer has an ongoing strategic review, and CRM/Neuromodulation is a major segment. Watch for any strategic review updates or segment-level announcements.

watchToday.text: ON/SYNA S-4 filing with deal terms (price, structure, synergy guidance) — the key catalyst for the semi complex
FDS reports earnings July 1 — will confirm or refute the AI-agent re-rating thesis
CAPR FDA AdCom on July 29 for Deramiocel BLA — briefing documents typically 1-2 days prior
SNDK and MU near-dated options ($1500 and $1200 3dte calls) — resolution will signal storage/semi direction
SEM and AES take-private closing conditions (regulatory approvals remaining) — watch for DOJ/antitrust or CFIUS updates
NFG Vectren Ohio acquisition expected to close Q4 2026 — watch for FERC or additional state regulatory approvals
